exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.description: Deadlifts help build overall strength and target muscles in the back, legs, and core.
exercises.tips: Keep your back straight while lifting.
steps: Stand with your feet hip-width apart.
Grip the barbell just outside your knees.
Engage your core and pull the barbell up by driving through your heels.
Keep the bar close to your body as you stand upright.
Lower the bar slowly back to the ground

exercises.name: Bench Press
exercises.description: The bench press is a classic exercise for building upper body strength, especially the chest.
exercises.tips: Don't let your elbows flare out too much.
steps: Lie flat on the bench with your feet flat on the floor.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Lower the bar slowly to your chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Press the bar back up to arm’s length, focusing on squeezing your chest.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.description: Leg press machines target your quads, hamstrings, and glutes for lower body strength.
exercises.tips: Ensure your feet are placed evenly on the platform.
steps: Sit down on the leg press machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Place your feet flat on the platform, ensuring your knees are in line with your toes.
Push the platform away with your heels, extending your legs.
Slowly lower the weight back down while maintaining control.

exercises.name: Pull-up
exercises.description: Pull-ups help strengthen the back, shoulders, and arms by engaging multiple muscle groups.
exercises.tips: Engage your core and avoid swinging.
steps: Grab the pull-up bar with palms facing away and hands shoulder-width apart.
Hang with your arms fully extended.
Pull your body upwards until your chin is above the bar.
Slowly lower your body back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Overhead Press
exercises.description: The overhead press primarily targets the shoulders, triceps, and upper chest.
exercises.tips: Avoid arching your back too much.
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and grip the bar at shoulder height.
Press the bar overhead while keeping your core tight.
Lower the bar slowly back to shoulder height.

exercises.name: Bicep Curl
exercises.description: Bicep curls effectively help strengthen and target the muscles in the arms.
exercises.tips: Don't swing your body during the movement.
steps: Stand with a dumbbell in each hand, arms fully extended.
Curl the dumbbells towards your shoulders by bending your elbows.
Lower the dumbbells back down slowly.

exercises.name: Tricep Dips
exercises.description: Tricep dips intensely improve strength and target the triceps, helping with arm strength.
exercises.tips: Keep your elbows close to your body.
steps: Place your hands behind you on a bench or dip bars.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Press back up to the starting position.

exercises.name: Lunges
exercises.description: Lunges are excellent for targeting the legs, especially the quads and glutes.
exercises.tips: Keep your chest up and your knees behind your toes.
steps: Step forward with one leg, lowering your hips to a 90-degree angle.
Push off the front foot and return to standing position.
Repeat on the other leg.

exercises.name: Leg Curl Machine
exercises.description: Leg curls effectively target the hamstrings to help boost mobility, improve flexibility, and increase endurance while enhancing lower body strength.
exercises.tips: Control the movement, especially on the return phase.
steps: Sit down on the leg curl machine with your legs extended and ankles under the pad.
Curl your legs towards your glutes by bending your knees.
Slowly return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
exercises.description: Goblet squats are a great variation of the squat that help improve lower body strength and mobility, while also engaging the core.
exercises.tips: Keep the weight close to your chest and maintain a tall posture throughout.
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell or kettlebell close to your chest with both hands.
Engage your core and keep your chest upright.
Push your hips back and bend your knees to lower into a squat position.
Ensure your knees are in line with your toes and do not cave in.
Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the ground, then drive through your heels to return to standing.

exercises.name: Chest Fly
exercises.description: Chest flys target the chest and shoulders to build upper body strength, improve posture, and enhance muscle definition and stability.
exercises.tips: Avoid locking your elbows during the movement.
steps: Lie on a bench holding a pair of dumbbells with your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells outward to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Bring the dumbbells back together, squeezing your chest at the top.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.description: The lat pulldown targets the back muscles, particularly the lats, to help with upper body strength, improve posture, and enhance muscle development.
exercises.tips: Focus on pulling your elbows down, not your hands.
steps: Sit at the lat pulldown machine with your feet flat on the floor.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width with palms facing forward.
Pull the bar down towards your chest while squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Slowly release the bar back to the starting position with control.

exercises.name: Push-ups
exercises.description: Push-ups are a bodyweight exercise that primarily target the chest, shoulders, and triceps.
exercises.tips: Keep your body straight and core engaged throughout the movement.
steps: Start in a high plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lower your body until your chest almost touches the ground.
Push through your palms to raise your body back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Squats
exercises.description: Squats target your lower body, specifically your quads, hamstrings, and glutes.
exercises.tips: Ensure your knees don't go past your toes and keep your back straight.
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and lower your body as if sitting in a chair.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, then return to standing.

exercises.name: Lunges
exercises.description: Lunges target your legs, specifically your quads, hamstrings, glutes, and calves.
exercises.tips: Ensure your knee doesn't pass your toes and your back stays straight.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart.
Step forward with one leg and lower your body until both knees are at 90-degree angles.
Push through the front heel to return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.description: Planks strengthen your core, shoulders, back, arms, legs, and stability, while also improving posture, balance, and endurance.
exercises.tips: Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
steps: Start in a forearm plank position with elbows under your shoulders.
Engage your core and keep your body straight.
Hold this position for the desired time.

exercises.name: Jump Squats
exercises.description: Jump squats are a plyometric exercise that targets the quads, glutes, and calves while improving explosive power.
exercises.tips: Ensure a soft landing to protect your knees and maintain control.
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a squat position, ensuring your knees don't pass your toes.
Explode upward, jumping as high as you can.
Land softly and immediately lower back into the squat position for the next jump.

exercises.name: High Knees
exercises.description: High knees increase your heart rate, work your legs, and improve cardiovascular endurance while engaging your core and boosting overall stamina.
exercises.tips: Drive your knees up high and maintain a quick pace.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart.
Quickly alternate bringing your knees toward your chest.
Maintain a steady pace and keep your core engaged.

exercises.name: Jumping Jacks
exercises.description: Jumping jacks are a full-body exercise that also increases heart rate, enhancing coordination, improving muscular endurance, and boosting metabolism.
exercises.tips: Land softly to reduce impact on your joints.
steps: Stand tall with your arms by your sides.
Jump while spreading your legs and raising your arms overhead.
Jump back to the starting position and repeat.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.description: Glute bridges target the glutes, hamstrings, and core, helping to strengthen the lower body, improve posture, enhance flexibility, and promote stability.
exercises.tips: Push through your heels and squeeze your glutes at the top.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Lift your hips toward the ceiling, squeezing your glutes.
Lower your hips back to the ground and repeat.

exercises.name: Tricep Dips
exercises.description: Tricep dips target the triceps and shoulders, enhancing upper body strength, endurance, and toning.
exercises.tips: Keep your elbows close to your body and avoid shrugging your shoulders.
steps: Place your hands on a bench or chair behind you.
Lower your body by bending your elbows to about 90 degrees.
Push through your palms to return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Bicycle Crunches
exercises.description: Bicycle crunches work your abs and obliques, improving core strength, stability, and muscle definition.
exercises.tips: Make sure to twist your torso while keeping your back flat on the ground.
steps: Lie on your back with hands behind your head.
Lift your legs to a 90-degree angle.
Bring your elbow toward the opposite knee, straightening the other leg out.
Switch sides and repeat the movement.

exercises.name: Russian Twists
exercises.description: Russian twists target your obliques and core, improving strength, stability, balance, and rotational movement.
exercises.tips: Engage your core and rotate your torso, not just your arms.
steps: Sit on the ground with your knees bent and feet flat.
Lean back slightly and lift your feet off the ground.
Twist your torso to one side, then to the other.

exercises.name: Mountain Climbers
exercises.description: Mountain climbers target your core, shoulders, and legs, boosting cardiovascular health, agility, and overall endurance.
exercises.tips: Maintain a fast pace and keep your core engaged.
steps: Start in a high plank position.
Bring one knee toward your chest, then quickly switch legs.
Repeat the movement as quickly as possible.
